title: daucus communis subsp. bocconei
intro: Daucus communis subsp. bocconei, also known as wild carrot or Queen Anne's lace, is a subspecies of the wild carrot plant, native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anthelmintic, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Daucus communis subsp. bocconei include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and soothe respiratory problems. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional herbal medicine to treat various health conditions.
